summary: Fixed agent cancellation so partial responses are preserved. Root cause: when the user cancelled, the backend returned AvaError::Cancelled ('Agent run cancelled by user'), the frontend treated it as a plain error and discarded the in-flight streaming content from rustAgent.streamingContent(). Fix: (1) In useAgent.ts::run(), detect the cancellation error string and save partialContent + partialThinking as a regular assistant message with metadata.cancelled=true, then add a subtle 'Session interrupted' indicator instead of a red error. (2) Added 'cancelled' as a valid MessageError type in src/types/index.ts. (3) Updated ErrorRow.tsx to render cancelled messages as a muted italic indicator with a Ban icon rather than a red bordered error box with a Retry button. The TUI backend was already handling cancellation correctly (mark_interrupted_messages + preserving session messages); only the desktop frontend needed fixing.
root_cause: In Tauri mode, submit_goal throws when cancel fires. useAgent.ts::run() received errorText='Agent run cancelled by user', treated it as a generic error, and only added an error message — discarding all streaming content accumulated in rustAgent.streamingContent() and rustAgent.thinkingContent() signals. Those signals are not cleared until the next run() starts.
behaviour_before: User cancels → LiveStreamingBlock hides (isRunning=false) → All thinking/tool-calls/partial text lost → Red 'Agent run cancelled by user' error message shown with a Retry button
behaviour_after: User cancels → Partial assistant message with all streamed content preserved in session store → Subtle muted 'Session interrupted' indicator shown → No alarming red error box
